label: Protests erupt in Brussels over health equity provisions in global Pandemic Agreement
label_zh: 布魯塞爾因全球大流行病協議中的健康公平條款爆發抗議

timeline.title: Health Advocates Protest in Brussels Demanding Binding Equity Provisions in Final Pandemic Agreement Annex
timeline.title_zh: 健康倡導者在布魯塞爾抗議，要求最終大流行病協議附件包含具有約束力的公平條款
timeline.sub_title: Demonstrators led by AHF Europe gathered near the European Commission as negotiations enter a critical final phase, warning that current EU proposals risk repeating COVID-era vaccine inequalities.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 由 AHF Europe 領導的示威者在歐洲委員會附近集會，談判進入關鍵的最後階段，並警告稱歐盟目前的提議可能會重蹈 COVID 時期疫苗不平等的覆轍。
timeline.summary:
Protests erupted in the heart of Brussels on March 18, 2026, as health advocates and civil society organizations intensified their pressure on the European Union to stop blocking health equity provisions in the global Pandemic Agreement. Led by the AIDS Healthcare Foundation (AHF) Europe, demonstrators gathered on Archimedes Street near the European Commission headquarters, followed by a press conference at the Press Club Brussels Europe. [ref:4] The activists are demanding that European decision-makers support a legally binding Pathogen Access and Benefit-Sharing (PABS) Annex, which remains the final significant hurdle to the full implementation of the treaty.

The global Pandemic Agreement was originally adopted in May 2025, but its ratification is currently stalled pending the finalization of the PABS Annex. This mechanism is designed to govern how countries share samples of dangerous pathogens and their genetic sequence data in exchange for equitable access to the vaccines, diagnostics, and treatments developed from that data. While [ref:6, 4, 2, 3, 5] more than 80 developing nations—organized as the Group for Equity—are pushing for mandatory benefit-sharing and technology transfers, advocates claim that wealthy nations including Germany, Switzerland, and the broader EU bloc are resisting these binding commitments in favor of voluntary measures.

During the Brussels demonstration, health leaders warned that the current European proposal would only guarantee that 20% of pandemic-related health products are set aside for global distribution, with only 10% provided as donations. Critics argue this effectively preserves a "VIP line" for wealthy nations, mirroring the vaccine hoarding and distribution delays that characterized the COVID-19 pandemic. AHF representatives [ref:1] emphasized that without a legally enforceable framework, low- and middle-income countries will remain dependent on the charity of the Global North rather than achieving true health sovereignty and regional manufacturing capacity.

The timing of the protests is critical as the Intergovernmental Working Group (IGWG) is scheduled to begin a final round of intensive negotiations at the World Health Organization headquarters in Geneva next week. These talks are expected to run late into the night as member states attempt to bridge the vast divide between commercial interests and public health necessities before the May 2026 deadline for the 79th World Health Assembly. WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus expressed cautious optimism earlier this week, stating that while significant gaps remain, he believes "landing zones" for a consensus are emerging.

Adding to the pressure, Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) also issued a call for the EU to engage more constructively, noting that the ratification of the entire agreement rests on the success of these final PABS negotiations. As the world watches the developments in Geneva and Brussels, the outcome of the next few weeks will determine whether the international community has truly learned the lessons of 2020 or if the legal framework for future pandemic responses will remain fundamentally skewed toward the interests of the highest bidders.
timeline.summary_zh:
2026 年 3 月 18 日，布魯塞爾市中心爆發了抗議活動，健康倡導者和公民社會組織加大了對歐盟的壓力，要求其停止阻撓全球大流行病協議中的健康公平條款。由愛滋病醫療基金會（AHF）歐洲分會領導的示威者聚集在歐洲委員會總部附近的阿基米德街，隨後在布魯塞爾歐洲新聞俱樂部舉行了新聞發布會。活動人士要求歐洲決策者支持一份具有法律約束力的病原體獲取與惠益分享（PABS）附件，這仍然是全面實施該條約的最後一個重大障礙。 [ref:4]

全球大流行病協議最初於 2025 年 5 月通過，但其批准目前懸而未決，取決於 PABS 附件的最終確定。該機制旨在規範各國如何分享危險病原體樣本及其基因序列數據，以換取對從該數據開發的疫苗、診斷工具和治療方法的公平獲取。雖然 80 多個發展中國家（以「公平集團」組織）正在推動強制性的惠益分享和技術轉讓，但倡導者聲稱，包括德國、瑞士和廣泛的歐盟集團在內的富裕國家正在抵制這些具有約束力的承諾，傾向於自願措施。 [ref:6, 4, 2, 3, 5]

在布魯塞爾的示威活動中，健康領袖警告稱，歐洲目前的提議僅能確保 20% 的大流行病相關衛生產品將被保留用於全球分配，其中只有 10% 作為捐贈。批評者認為，這實際上為富裕國家保留了「VIP通道」，這與 COVID-19 大流行病期間疫苗囤積和分發延誤的情況如出一轍。AHF 代表強調，沒有具有法律執行力的框架，中低收入國家將繼續依賴北方國家的慈善，而不是實現真正的健康主權和區域製造能力。 [ref:1]

這次抗議活動的時間點至關重要，因為政府間工作組（IGWG）將於下週在世界衛生組織總部日內瓦開始新一輪的密集談判。預計這些談判將持續到深夜，因為成員國將在 2026 年 5 月第 79 屆世界衛生大會的截止日期前，努力彌合商業利益與公共衛生需求之間的巨大分歧。世衛組織總幹事譚德塞博士本週早些時候對此表示謹慎樂觀，他說儘管仍然存在重大差距，但他相信正在出現達成共識的「著力點」。

此外，無國界醫生（MSF）也呼籲歐盟進行更具建設性的參與，指出整個協議的批准取決於這些最終 PABS 談判的成功。隨著世界關注日內瓦和布魯塞爾的事態發展，未來幾週的結果將決定國際社會是否真正吸取了 2020 年的教訓，還是未來大流行病應對的法律框架將繼續嚴重偏向最高出價者的利益。

timeline.title: Advocacy Groups Launch Brussels Campaign Urging EU to End Stalemate Over Global Pandemic Health Equity
timeline.title_zh: 倡導團體發起布魯塞爾運動，敦促歐盟結束全球大流行病健康公平僵局
timeline.sub_title: Advocacy groups are intensifying pressure on the European Commission to support legally binding benefit-sharing rules as negotiations for the WHO Pandemic Agreement reach a critical deadline.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 隨著世界衛生組織大流行病協定談判進入關鍵期限，倡導團體正加強對歐洲聯盟委員會的壓力，敦促其支持具有法律約束力的利益分享規則。
timeline.summary:
On March 17, 2026, the AIDS Healthcare Foundation (AHF) Europe and a coalition of civil society organizations escalated their advocacy efforts in Brussels, demanding that the European Union cease its opposition to robust health equity provisions within the global Pandemic Agreement. This surge in activity comes as the May 2026 deadline for finalizing the Pathogen Access and Benefit-Sharing (PABS) Annex approaches, a component deemed essential for the treaty's ratification. [ref:2] Protesters and health advocates argue that without a legally binding framework for the equitable distribution of vaccines and therapeutics, the global community risks a repeat of the inequities seen during the COVID-19 pandemic.

The current stalemate centers on the PABS system, which was left as an unfinished annex when the broader Pandemic Agreement was adopted by the World Health Assembly in May 2025. While the primary text of the treaty is currently 'frozen in place,' it cannot be opened for signature or implementation until the specifics of the PABS Annex are negotiated. Developing [ref:1] nations and advocacy groups are calling for a system where the sharing of pathogen samples and genetic sequence data by low-income countries is met with guaranteed, mandatory access to a percentage of the resulting medical products. Specifically, they are pushing for a requirement that manufacturers donate at least 10% of their pandemic-related products to the WHO and provide another 10% at non-profit prices.

However, the European Commission, alongside high-income nations such as Germany and Switzerland, has reportedly resisted these mandatory obligations. Negotiators from these regions have favored voluntary arrangements and 'mutually agreed' terms for technology transfers, a stance that critics claim protects pharmaceutical profit margins at the expense of global health security. The 'EU: Stop Blocking Health Equity' campaign has specifically targeted the European Commission's headquarters in Brussels, organizing advocacy actions on Archimedes Street and hosting press conferences to highlight what they describe as 'extraction-based' cooperation rather than genuine partnership.

This targeted activism follows a broader week of social unrest in the Belgian capital. On March 16, healthcare workers and trade unions held demonstrations near European institutions to protest the commercialization of healthcare and deteriorating working conditions. While those protests focused on domestic and regional issues, they have coalesced with the global health equity movement to create a unified front against the prioritization of corporate interests over public services. Activists have adopted the slogan 'No Equity, No Agreement,' signaling their intent to oppose any final version of the PABS Annex that lacks enforceable accountability mechanisms.

As the 79th World Health Assembly looms in May, the pressure on Brussels to compromise is mounting. Advocates warn that a weak annex would not only undermine efforts to build regional manufacturing capacity in the Global South but also leave the world vulnerable to future pathogens. The outcome of the current negotiations in Brussels and Geneva will determine whether the world’s first unified pandemic preparedness legal instrument can finally move from a draft proposal to a functional, ratified international law.
timeline.summary_zh:
2026年3月17日，愛滋病醫療基金會（AHF）歐洲分部及一眾公民社會組織在布魯塞爾加強了其倡導行動，要求歐洲聯盟停止反對全球大流行病協定中的健全健康公平條款。此舉正值病原體獲取與利益分享（PABS）附件的 finalization 截止日期（2026年5月）臨近之際，該附件被認為是條約批准的關鍵組成部分。抗議者和健康倡導者認為，若沒有一個具有法律約束力的公平分配疫苗和治療方法的框架，全球社會將面臨重演COVID-19大流行期間不公現象的風險。[ref:2, 1]

目前的僵局集中在PABS系統，該系統在2025年5月世界衛生大會通過更廣泛的大流行病協定時，仍被留為一個未完成的附件。雖然條約的主要文本目前處於「凍結狀態」，但在PABS附件細節談判完成之前，它無法開放簽署或實施。發展中國家和倡導團體呼籲建立一個體系，要求低收入國家分享病原體樣本和基因序列數據的同時，能獲得相當比例的 الناتج 醫療產品的保證性、強制性獲取。具體而言，他們正推動要求製造商將其大流行病相關產品至少10%捐贈給世界衛生組織，並以非營利價格提供另外10%。

然而，據報導，歐洲聯盟委員會以及德國和瑞士等高收入國家一直抵制這些強制性義務。這些地區的談判代表傾向於採取自願安排和「相互協定」的技術轉讓條款，批評者認為這種立場是以犧牲全球健康安全為代價來保護製藥公司的利潤。 「歐盟：停止阻礙健康公平」運動特別針對設在布魯塞爾的歐洲聯盟委員會總部，在阿基米德街組織了倡導活動，並召開新聞發布會，強調他們所描述的「剝削式」合作，而非真正的夥伴關係。

這種有針對性的行動發生在比利時首都一系列廣泛的社會動盪之後。3月16日，醫護人員和工會 Near 歐洲機構舉行了示威活動，抗議醫療商業化和工作條件惡化。雖然這些抗議主要集中在國內和區域性問題，但它們與全球健康公平運動匯合，形成了一個反對將企業利益置於公共服務之上的統一陣線。活躍人士採納了「沒有公平，就沒有協定」的口號，表明他們反對任何缺乏可強制執行問責機制的PABS附件最終版本的意圖。

隨著第79屆世界衛生大會將於5月召開，布魯塞爾面臨的妥協壓力日益增加。倡導者警告稱，一份軟弱的附件不僅會破壞在全球南方建立區域製造能力的努力，還會使世界容易受到未來病原體的威脅。目前在布魯塞爾和日內瓦的談判結果，將決定世界上第一個統一的流行病防備法律文書能否最終從草案提案轉變為一項功能性、已批准的國際法。
